title: Saudi Chip Design and SSIH
entry_mechanism: PSAP Track 9 — SSIH (Saudi Semiconductor and Industrial Hub) established by GADD with Aramco and PIF as co-investors
executive_summary:
The global semiconductor industry — designing and manufacturing the chips that power every electronic device — is dominated by a small number of nations and companies. Saudi Arabia has no domestic semiconductor design or manufacturing capability, creating total dependency on imports from TSMC, Samsung, Intel, and their supply chains. Saudi Arabia's National Semiconductor Strategy 2026–2035 targets the establishment of a Saudi Semiconductor and Industrial Hub (SSIH) — beginning with chip design capability before moving to manufacturing in later phases.

Node SC-1 targets Phase I SSIH chip design: establishing a Saudi semiconductor design organisation capable of designing custom chips for Saudi government and military applications — initially in non-sensitive commercial domains (industrial IoT, edge AI inference, automotive) before progressing to classified defence applications. The design tools are commercially available (Synopsys, Cadence EDA tools); the constraint is human capital — Saudi VLSI design engineers.

The global semiconductor market was valued at $600 billion in 2024 growing at 8.8% CAGR. Saudi Arabia's Phase I chip design target — custom chips for Saudi-specific applications — addresses a $50–100 million annual import replacement opportunity initially, with dramatically larger implications as Saudi manufacturing capability develops.

companies.name: Synopsys
companies.rationale: Synopsys provides the EDA (Electronic Design Automation) tools used to design chips. Saudi government licensing and Synopsys training programme for Saudi design engineers.

companies.name: Arm Holdings
companies.rationale: Arm licenses its processor architectures (used in 95% of smartphones and growing share of AI chips). Saudi chip design based on Arm architecture is the most commercially logical path.

companies.name: RISC-V International
companies.rationale: RISC-V is an open-source chip architecture with no licensing fees — strategically valuable for a sovereign chip programme that doesn't want IP dependency on Arm.

companies.name: Intel Foundry (formerly IFS)
companies.rationale: Intel Foundry provides chip manufacturing services and design support for fabless companies.

companies.name: GlobalFoundries
companies.rationale: GlobalFoundries manufactures chips for fabless design companies and has Gulf investor relationships (ATIC was a major shareholder). Less advanced node but accessible.

partners.name: GADD
partners.current: Defence R&D; some electronics research; SSIH programme planning
partners.upgrade: GADD as SSIH programme authority; EDA tool procurement; hire 50 Saudi chip design engineers (GADD fellowship at Arm, Intel, Qualcomm); first Saudi chip tape-out in 36 months
partners.timeline: 36 months to first Saudi chip design

partners.name: Saudi Aramco Digital
partners.current: AI and IoT technology investment; HPC infrastructure
partners.upgrade: Aramco edge AI chip requirements drive Saudi chip design; Aramco as anchor customer for Saudi-designed industrial IoT chip; Aramco co-investment in SSIH design centre

partners.name: KACST Microelectronics Research
partners.current: Some semiconductor research; CMOS design research
partners.upgrade: GADD-KACST chip design research programme; Saudi VLSI research laboratory; academic chip design pipeline; KACST first Saudi chip tape-out on university programme

partners.name: KFUPM / KAUST Electrical Engineering
partners.current: Electrical engineering; digital systems design; some VLSI courses
partners.upgrade: VLSI design engineering programme; Synopsys and Cadence university programme; Saudi chip design engineer pipeline (20 per year); SSIH design centre graduate intake
